title: Power Query M Language Reference — Syntax, Functions, SharePoint CSV Patterns

confirmed_facts.fact: M queries use let/in structure: each line in 'let' is a named step (lazy evaluation), 'in' specifies the final output. Steps are named expressions, not sequential instructions.
'each' is shorthand for (_) => where _ is the implicit parameter. Inside 'each', [ColumnName] accesses the current row's field. each [Amount] > 100 is equivalent to (_) => _[Amount] > 100.

SharePoint.Files(url, [ApiVersion=15]) connects to a SharePoint site and returns ALL files in ALL document libraries recursively. URL must be root site only (no library path). Returns columns: Content (binary), Name, Extension, Date modified, Folder Path.
Csv.Document(binary, [Delimiter, Columns, Encoding, QuoteStyle]) parses CSV binary into a table. Encoding 65001=UTF-8, 1252=Windows Latin-1. QuoteStyle.Csv handles quoted fields with commas.
Table.UnpivotOtherColumns(table, fixedColumns, attributeName, valueName) unpivots all columns except the fixed ones — robust when new metric columns appear in newer files.
M type system: text, number, Int64.Type, Currency.Type, date, datetime, datetimezone, duration, logical, list, table, record, binary, null. Date literals use #date(YYYY, M, D) syntax.
Error handling: try expression otherwise defaultValue. Example: try Number.FromText([Value]) otherwise 0.

implementation_notes:
Text functions:
```m
Text.Contains([Folder Path], "/Reports/")     // substring check
Text.StartsWith([Name], "report_")             // prefix check
Text.Split("2024-01", "-")                     // returns {"2024", "01"}
Text.Middle("report_2024_01.csv", 7, 4)         // returns "2024" (zero-based offset)
Text.PadStart(Text.From(5), 2, "0")            // returns "05"
Text.Replace("hello-world", "-", "_")          // returns "hello_world"
Text.BetweenDelimiters("[Category]", "[", "]") // returns "Category"
Text.From(2024)                                // number to text: "2024"
```

Date functions:
```m
Date.Year(#date(2024, 3, 15))          // 2024
Date.Month(#date(2024, 3, 15))         // 3
Date.QuarterOfYear(#date(2024, 3, 15)) // 1
Date.StartOfMonth(#date(2024, 3, 15))  // #date(2024, 3, 1)
Date.EndOfMonth(#date(2024, 2, 1))     // #date(2024, 2, 29)
Date.AddMonths(#date(2024, 1, 15), 1)  // #date(2024, 2, 15)
Date.FromText("2024-01-15")
Date.ToText(#date(2024, 3, 15), "MMM YYYY") // "Mar 2024"
Date.From(DateTime.LocalNow())         // today's date

// Generate list of all dates
List.Dates(#date(2023, 1, 1), 365, #duration(1, 0, 0, 0))

// Days between two dates
Duration.Days(#date(2024,12,31) - #date(2024,1,1))  // 365
```

SharePoint CSV combine template (report_YYYY_MM.csv):
```m
let
    Source = SharePoint.Files("https://contoso.sharepoint.com/sites/MySite", [ApiVersion=15]),
    InFolder = Table.SelectRows(Source, each Text.Contains([Folder Path], "/Shared Documents/Reports/")),
    CSVFiles = Table.SelectRows(InFolder, each [Extension] = ".csv" and not Text.StartsWith([Name], "~")),
    WithFile = Table.AddColumn(CSVFiles, "SourceFile", each [Name], type text),
    WithYear = Table.AddColumn(WithFile, "FileYear",
        each try Number.FromText(Text.Middle([SourceFile], 7, 4)) otherwise null, Int64.Type),
    WithMonth = Table.AddColumn(WithYear, "FileMonth",
        each try Number.FromText(Text.Middle([SourceFile], 12, 2)) otherwise null, Int64.Type),
    Valid = Table.SelectRows(WithMonth, each [FileYear] <> null and [FileMonth] <> null),
    Parsed = Table.AddColumn(Valid, "_Data",
        each Csv.Document([Content], [Delimiter=",", Encoding=65001, QuoteStyle=QuoteStyle.Csv])),
    Headers = Table.TransformColumns(Parsed, {"_Data", each Table.PromoteHeaders(_, [PromoteAllScalars=true])}),
    Expanded = Table.ExpandTableColumn(Headers, "_Data", Table.ColumnNames(Headers{0}[_Data])),
    WithDate = Table.AddColumn(Expanded, "MonthDate",
        each try #date([FileYear], [FileMonth], 1) otherwise null, type date),
    Trimmed = Table.RemoveColumns(WithDate,
        {"Content", "Extension", "Date accessed", "Date modified", "Date created", "Attributes", "Folder Path"})
in
    Trimmed
```

Custom function pattern for reusable CSV parsing:
```m
// Query named: fnParseMonthlyCSV
(csvBinary as binary, fileName as text) as table =>
let
    Raw     = Csv.Document(csvBinary, [Delimiter=",", Encoding=65001, QuoteStyle=QuoteStyle.Csv]),
    Headers = Table.PromoteHeaders(Raw, [PromoteAllScalars=true]),
    AddFile = Table.AddColumn(Headers, "SourceFile", each fileName, type text),
    AddYear = Table.AddColumn(AddFile, "FileYear",
        each Number.FromText(Text.Middle(fileName, 7, 4)), Int64.Type),
    AddMonth= Table.AddColumn(AddYear, "FileMonth",
        each Number.FromText(Text.Middle(fileName, 12, 2)), Int64.Type),
    AddDate = Table.AddColumn(AddMonth, "MonthDate",
        each #date([FileYear], [FileMonth], 1), type date)
in
    AddDate

// Main query calls it:
let
    Source   = SharePoint.Files("https://contoso.sharepoint.com/sites/MySite", [ApiVersion=15]),
    Filtered = Table.SelectRows(Source,
        each Text.Contains([Folder Path], "/Reports/") and [Extension] = ".csv"),
    Invoked  = Table.AddColumn(Filtered, "_Parsed", each fnParseMonthlyCSV([Content], [Name])),
    Combined = Table.Combine(Invoked[_Parsed])
in
    Combined
```
caveats: SharePoint.Files URL must be root site only — including library path causes authentication errors
Filenames containing # % $ cause truncation in SharePoint URLs — rename files to remove these characters
Encoding 65001 (UTF-8) is correct for SharePoint Online files — use 1252 only for legacy Windows files
Table.UnpivotOtherColumns is preferred over Table.Unpivot when new metric columns may appear in future files
ExpandTableColumn requires all files to have matching schemas — varying column names cause errors
Text.Middle uses zero-based offsets — 'report_' is 7 chars, so year starts at offset 7
SharePoint folder connector returns files from all subfolders — always filter by Folder Path
